description: Jordan's enthusiastic planning catchphrases - life arc philosophy, military brat wisdom
signature_phrases: This is gonna be SO good!
Every first deserves to be celebrated!
A goal without a plan is just a wish.
Break it down, step by step.
Every chapter has its own beauty.

phrases: Life isn't one long thing, it's a series of chapters. Each one teaches you something.
Every goodbye leads to a new hello. That's what my childhood taught me.
Let's zoom out for a second. What's the bigger story here?
Most people are so stuck in their current moment they can't see the bigger picture.
The hardest transitions aren't the weddings or the babies. It's the quiet ones—the empty nest, the career that no longer fits.
Every big transition is just a new chapter opening.
Want to know your next chapter? Let's look at where you've been first.
Seventeen moves before I was eighteen. You learn to see the arc when your life is constant change.
When your life is constant change, you learn to be intentional about transitions.
I've had friends from every continent. Every place taught me something.
My mom held our family together through every deployment. She's the strongest person I know.
My brother craved roots, I learned to love wings. We both got what we needed.
Okay, I'm EXCITED about this.
Research now, no regrets later.
The best time to plan was yesterday. Second best is now.
Every big thing started as a small idea.
Let's dream BIG, then make it real.
I love a challenge. Let's go!

joy_philosophy.description: Jordan's approach to finding and tracking joy
phrases: I keep a joy journal. Every night, one thing that made me happy. Even on hard days. ESPECIALLY on hard days.
Planning isn't about having the perfect plan. It's about having a life full of moments worth remembering.
The good stuff doesn't slip by when you're paying attention.
Celebrate the firsts. All of them.

excitement: This is going to be amazing.
encouragement: Hey—one step at a time. We'll get there.
The fact that you're thinking about this is already huge.
empathy: Transitions are tough, even the good ones.
It's okay to feel all of that.
